results.question: Do genetically identical cells express the same gene at the same level?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names a specific per-cell expression observable (a protein/mRNA count or a mass channel), not 'behavior'
min_criteria.rationale: Uses ribosome_conc and rna_mass channels as per-seed expression proxies — concrete mass/conc observables.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts cell-to-cell variance > 0 relative to an explicit isogenic baseline
min_criteria.rationale: Predicts CV>=20% across isogenic wildtype/basal seeds vs a <10% deterministic-baseline expectation.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a concrete falsifier (e.g. variance indistinguishable from technical/replicate noise)
min_criteria.rationale: Refuting result: pooled CV <10% (3-5%) indicates near-deterministic replicates; explicit threshold given.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is an isogenic replicate ensemble (wildtype, many seeds), in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: Candidate designs are wildtype/basal, 20 seeds, minimal_GLC — isogenic replicate ensemble in-envelope.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: gives a quantitative dispersion statistic with a threshold (a CV or Fano, or intrinsic ~1/mean scaling)
stringent_criteria.rationale: Specifies CV=SD/mean with explicit thresholds (>=20% vs <10%) and mentions burst-size/1/mean scaling via KO control.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: names the statistic/test and how significance is judged (e.g. variance decomposition with CIs / Welch t)
stringent_criteria.rationale: Names Welch t-test (|t|<2) for batch drift plus CV thresholds and Pearson r for rival discrimination.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the predicted result discriminates intrinsic-vs-extrinsic from the pure-measurement-noise rival
stringent_criteria.rationale: Welch t batch check rules out RNG/measurement artifact, and CV>=20% vs technical <10% distinguishes real variability from measurement noise.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: includes the isolating control — the intrinsic/extrinsic decomposition (dual identical genes, or conditioning on shared ribosome/RNAP/mass covariates)
stringent_criteria.rationale: Rivals condition on dry_mass bins (mass covariate) and correlate with growth_rate/ppGpp (shared global covariate), operationalizing the intrinsic/extrinsic split via covariate conditioning as required by scope_note.

results.comment: Strong, well-operationalized hypothesis. All minimum criteria clearly met with concrete channels, thresholds, and falsifier. Stringent criteria also met: it supplies the intrinsic/extrinsic decomposition via mass-bin conditioning and growth_rate correlation (the scope-note-mandated proxy for the absent dual reporter), plus a burst-size KO control. Only weakness is that no native single-gene copy channel exists, honestly flagged as a caveat, but proxies are mapped in-model.

hypothesis.question: Do genetically identical cells express the same gene at the same level?
hypothesis.claim: Isogenic E. coli cells growing in the same static medium show substantial cell-to-cell variability in gene expression (measured here via ribosome concentration and RNA mass as proxies for expression output) due to intrinsic stochastic fluctuations in transcription/translation, rather than expressing at a single deterministic level.
hypothesis.h1: Across an isogenic population under identical (wildtype/basal) conditions, single-cell expression-proxy channels (ribosome_conc, rna_mass) exhibit coefficient of variation (CV) >= 20% across seeds at matched generation/timepoint, driven by intrinsic stochastic noise rather than measurement/replicate artifact.
hypothesis.h0: Isogenic cells under identical conditions express genes at essentially the same level: CV of ribosome_conc and rna_mass across seeds is < 10% (indistinguishable from technical/seed-init noise), i.e. expression is deterministic given genotype+environment.

operational_defs.observable: ribosome_conc and rna_mass channels sampled per-seed at a fixed generation/timepoint
operational_defs.measure: per-seed value; population CV = SD/mean across seeds

operational_defs.observable: variance in ribosome_conc/rna_mass across independent stochastic seeds run under IDENTICAL params/condition/timeline (only RNG seed differs)
operational_defs.measure: CV across seeds; compared against a matched-mean deterministic-burst control (rrna_operon_knockout at reduced copy) to test if variance scales with expected burst-size relationship

hypothesis.predicted_effect: Population CV of ribosome_conc and rna_mass across >=20 seeds at matched generation (e.g. generation 8) is >=20% (point estimate ~25-30%), i.e. at least 2-3x higher than the <10% CV expected from purely deterministic dynamics with only numerical/seed-init noise; distribution is unimodal but right-skewed (skewness > 0.5), not bimodal.

falsifier.decision_rule: Compute CV across >=20 seeds at generation 8 for wildtype/basal (target run) vs a held-out independent wildtype/basal reference batch of >=20 seeds; Welch t-test on per-seed ribosome_conc means between the two batches to confirm no systematic batch drift (expect |t|<2, non-significant, ruling out RNG-batch artifact); THEN test H1 by computing pooled CV across all 40+ seeds: if pooled CV >=20%, retain H1; if pooled CV <10%, reject H1 in favor of H0.
falsifier.refuting_result: Pooled CV of ribosome_conc across all seeds is <10% (e.g. 3-5%), indicating cells are near-deterministic replicates rather than intrinsically noisy expressors.

rivals.claim: Apparent variability is not intrinsic gene-expression noise but reflects deterministic cell-cycle-phase heterogeneity (cells sampled at same generation number but different absolute cell-cycle progression/mass at division).
rivals.distinguishing_result: If variability is a cell-cycle-phase artifact, CV of ribosome_conc computed within narrow dry_mass bins should collapse below 10%, while the unconditioned CV stays >=20%; intrinsic-noise hypothesis predicts CV stays >=15% even within tight mass bins.

rivals.claim: Variability is dominated by extrinsic global noise (e.g. shared fluctuations in ppGpp/growth_rate affecting all genes coordinately) rather than gene-intrinsic stochastic bursting.
rivals.distinguishing_result: If extrinsic/global noise dominates, ribosome_conc and rna_mass CVs should be tightly correlated with growth_rate CV across the same seeds (Pearson r>=0.7); intrinsic-noise hypothesis predicts weaker coupling (r<0.4). The rrna_operon_knockout control (halving ribosome gene copy number) should shift ribosome_conc CV substantially (per burst-size scaling, CV should rise, e.g. by >=1.3x relative to wildtype) while leaving growth_rate CV comparatively unchanged (<20% relative shift) if noise is gene-intrinsic; if instead growth_rate CV shifts proportionally with ribosome_conc CV, extrinsic global noise is favored.

auxiliary_assumptions: The simulator's per-seed RNG differences approximate biologically realistic intrinsic stochastic noise sources (transcription/translation bursting), not merely numerical integration artifacts.
Sampling at a fixed generation number (e.g. 8) across seeds is a valid proxy for sampling an isogenic population at a matched physiological timepoint, despite asynchronous division times.

ribosome_conc and rna_mass are adequate proxies for 'gene expression level' in the absence of a single-gene mRNA/protein copy-number channel; results generalize qualitatively to individual gene transcripts.

Whether the simulator implements stochastic transcription/translation with realistic burst kinetics (vs. quasi-deterministic ODE dynamics with only initial-condition noise) is not fully knowable a priori and is treated as a scope caveat rather than chased further.
The rrna_operon_knockout control at 50% dosage is assumed to reduce ribosome gene copy number without lethal growth arrest, permitting comparable generation counts.

results.question: How does a cell decide how many ribosomes to make in a given medium?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names ribosome/ribosomal fraction and growth rate as the observables
min_criteria.rationale: Operational defs explicitly use ribosome_conc and growth_rate channels.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts a positive ribosome-fraction vs growth-rate relation vs a 'constant content' null
min_criteria.rationale: H1 predicts positive linear ribosome_conc=a+b*growth_rate with b>0, R^2>=0.85; H0 provides a direct-tracking/passive-allocation null.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a falsifier (flat or negative relation under nutrient modulation)
min_criteria.rationale: Falsifier includes rejecting H1 if slope CI/direction fails and rich clamp-based rules; explicit refuting conditions given.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is steady-state sims across >=2 media conditions, in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: Three media (basal, acetate, succinate), 8 seeds, 6 generations, last-2-gen averaging; in-envelope perturbations.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: states the linear form with a slope/intercept and a target fit quality
stringent_criteria.rationale: ribosome_conc = a + b*growth_rate with b>0 and R^2>=0.85 target explicitly stated.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: specifies a regression with a CI on the slope across media
stringent_criteria.rationale: Falsifier invokes OLS regression with slope 95% CI across the three conditions.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the prediction discriminates the growth-law from nutrient-specific and one-way rivals
stringent_criteria.rationale: Rivals include ppGpp-independent nutrient-sensing (nutrient-specific-like) and one-directional stress-collapse; distinguishing results via bidirectional clamp discriminate them.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: includes the translation-inhibition arm to establish bidirectional coupling
stringent_criteria.rationale: Bidirectional ppGpp clamp (4x high in rich, 0.25x low in poor) plus relA knockout establishes bidirectional causal coupling analogous to translation-inhibition arm; captures the negative-arm coupling via elevated ppGpp reducing ribosomes.

results.comment: Strong, well-operationalized hypothesis. It recovers the Scott growth-law positive relation and adds a mechanistic ppGpp-causality layer with a genuine bidirectional clamp and quantitative regression/CI falsifiers. S4 is credited: rather than a literal antibiotic translation-inhibition arm, it uses the high-ppGpp clamp to depress ribosomes (the negative arm) and low-ppGpp clamp to raise them, establishing bidirectional coupling on the model's real channels. All criteria map to in-model observables (ribosome_conc, growth_rate, ppgpp_conc, fraction_trna_charged, protein_mass).

hypothesis.question: How does a cell decide how many ribosomes to make in a given medium?
hypothesis.claim: E. coli sets ribosome concentration as a ppGpp-mediated readout of amino-acid (tRNA charging) supply relative to demand: growth-rate-dependent ribosome content is not set directly by nutrient quality but is causally routed through ppGpp bidirectionally, such that clamping ppGpp HIGH in a rich medium or LOW in a poor medium overrides the medium-specific ribosome level predicted by the growth-rate-dependent ('growth law') relationship, in a dose-dependent (not merely stress-collapse) manner.
hypothesis.h1: Across media of different quality, steady-state ribosome_conc scales positively with growth_rate (growth-law slope) BECAUSE ppgpp_conc scales negatively with fraction_trna_charged and negatively sets ribosome_conc; experimentally clamping ppgpp_conc to a fixed multiple of its basal value overrides the medium-dependent variation in ribosome_conc and growth_rate in a monotonic dose-dependent way: a 4x-basal clamp in rich medium (basal) drives ribosome_conc DOWN toward acetate-like levels, AND a 0.25x-basal clamp in poor medium (acetate) drives ribosome_conc UP toward basal-like levels -- i.e. the clamp sets ribosome_conc according to the clamped ppGpp value, not according to the medium, in both directions.
hypothesis.h0: Ribosome_conc tracks growth_rate/medium quality directly (e.g. via a nutrient-sensing pathway independent of ppGpp, or via passive resource allocation), so clamping ppgpp_conc will NOT abolish the medium-dependent differences in ribosome_conc and growth_rate; the growth-law relationship persists under the ppGpp clamp regardless of clamp direction/magnitude.

operational_defs.measure: ribosome_conc channel (uM), averaged over last 2 generations of steady growth
fraction_trna_charged channel (frac), averaged over last 2 generations

operational_defs.observable: joint drop in growth_rate AND ribosome_conc AND protein_mass accumulation rate irrespective of clamp direction
operational_defs.measure: growth_rate and protein_mass channels compared between high-clamp and low-clamp conditions

hypothesis.predicted_effect: (1) Growth-law form: across >=3 basal-quality conditions (basal, acetate, succinate) ribosome_conc vs growth_rate follows a positive linear relation, ribosome_conc = a + b*growth_rate, with b>0 and R^2>=0.85. (2) ppGpp clamp dose-response, BIDIRECTIONAL: (2a) clamping ppgpp_conc to 4x basal in rich medium (basal) reduces ribosome_conc by >=40% and growth_rate by >=30% relative to unclamped basal, toward acetate-like values. (2b) clamping ppgpp_conc to 0.25x basal in poor medium (acetate) INCREASES ribosome_conc by >=30% and growth_rate by >=20% relative to unclamped acetate, toward basal-like values. The clamp collapses the medium-dependent spread in ribosome_conc to <=10% of its unclamped range, and effect direction tracks clamp direction (not a uniform stress-collapse in both cases).

falsifier.decision_rule: Welch t-test of ribosome_conc, ppgpp_conc/acetate (clamped 0.25x, i.e. LOW clamp in poor medium) vs wildtype/acetate: reject H1 if |t|<2 OR effect_pct<20% in the predicted UP direction (i.e. low clamp fails to raise ribosome_conc). Also require ppgpp_conc/basal (4x clamp) vs wildtype/basal to show the predicted DOWN direction with |t|>=2 and effect_pct>=20%. If the HIGH clamp lowers ribosome_conc/growth_rate but the LOW clamp does NOT raise them (i.e. both clamps only ever depress growth_rate and protein_mass, consistent with generic stress-collapse regardless of direction), reject H1 in favor of the nonspecific-dormancy rival. Additionally reject H1 if OLS regression of ribosome_conc on growth_rate across the three unclamped wildtype conditions under a matched clamp still yields slope 95% CI excluding 0 with R^2>=0.85.
falsifier.refuting_result: ppgpp_conc/acetate (0.25x clamp) shows ribosome_conc within 20% of wildtype/acetate or moving in the wrong (down) direction, while ppgpp_conc/basal (4x clamp) does lower ribosome_conc -- i.e. only the high clamp produces an effect, consistent with a one-directional stress/dormancy response rather than bidirectional causal setting of ribosome level by ppGpp; and/or the growth-law slope survives clamping.

rivals.claim: Ribosome content is set by a ppGpp-independent nutrient-sensing/regulatory pathway (e.g. direct cAMP-CRP or ribosomal-protein autogenous feedback keyed to translation elongation rate), with ppGpp merely a downstream correlate, not the causal lever.
rivals.distinguishing_result: Under BOTH the high clamp (4x, basal) and the low clamp (0.25x, acetate), ribosome_conc STILL tracks growth_rate/medium in the direction predicted by unclamped medium quality (i.e. clamped-basal ribosome_conc stays high, clamped-acetate ribosome_conc stays low) despite ppGpp being pinned to non-medium-matched values -- the growth law survives the bidirectional clamp, refuting ppGpp-causality.

rivals.claim: High ppGpp triggers a generic, one-directional stress/dormancy shutdown program (not a bidirectional rheostat): elevating ppGpp above basal always suppresses ribosome_conc and growth_rate regardless of medium, but lowering ppGpp below basal in a poor medium does NOT relieve this suppression or raise ribosome_conc, because ribosome synthesis in poor medium is limited by something else (e.g. raw amino-acid/precursor availability, fraction_trna_charged) that a ppGpp clamp cannot bypass.
rivals.distinguishing_result: ppgpp_conc/acetate (0.25x clamp, LOW) fails to raise ribosome_conc and growth_rate above unclamped wildtype/acetate levels (|t|<2, effect_pct<20%), even though ppgpp_conc/basal (4x clamp, HIGH) does lower ribosome_conc/growth_rate substantially -- an asymmetric, one-directional response inconsistent with bidirectional ppGpp-set allocation.

rivals.claim: RelA/SpoT synthesis capacity (not tRNA charging per se) sets ppGpp and thus ribosome level; a relA gene_knockout should blunt but not eliminate the medium-dependent ppGpp response, with a quantitatively intermediate slope between full wildtype sensitivity and complete insensitivity.
rivals.distinguishing_result: Comparing relA/basal vs relA/acetate (paired growth_rate and ppgpp_conc measurements) to wildtype/basal vs wildtype/acetate: define the wildtype ppgpp_conc fold-change (basal-to-acetate) as 100% sensitivity and a hypothetical no-response as 0%; relA knockout shows a ppgpp_conc fold-change between 20% and 80% of the wildtype fold-change (a genuinely intermediate, numerically bounded value), together with a correspondingly intermediate ribosome_conc fold-change -- rather than either <=20% (near-full RelA-dependence) or >=80% (near-full ppGpp-independence of RelA specifically, i.e. SpoT alone suffices).

results.question: What does a cell do when it suddenly runs out of amino acids?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names ppGpp concentration and rRNA/ribosome or growth rate as observables
min_criteria.rationale: Explicitly uses ppgpp_conc, ribosome_conc, and growth_rate as observables in the operational definitions.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts ppGpp up and ribosome/growth down on downshift vs a steady-state baseline
min_criteria.rationale: Predicts >=5x ppGpp rise and >=40% ribosome / >=50% growth-rate drop vs pre-shift/wildtype basal baseline.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a falsifier (no ppGpp rise, or ribosome synthesis unchanged despite a spike)
min_criteria.rationale: Falsifier: ppGpp fold-change <2x (or |t|<2) refutes H1; explicit decision rule provided.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is an amino-acid downshift, in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: Design is amino_acid withdrawal timeline (supplemented -> withdrawn), a valid in-envelope downshift.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: gives the sign and rough magnitude/timescale of the ppGpp spike and ribosome drop
stringent_criteria.rationale: States ~5-10x ppGpp rise within 10-20 min and >=40% ribosome / >=50% growth drop within 30-60 min.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: specifies a pre/post (time-resolved) comparison statistic
stringent_criteria.rationale: Uses peak fold-change over 0-20 min window vs pre-shift baseline with a Welch t-test across seeds.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the prediction discriminates the active-ppGpp-signal from the passive-depletion rival
stringent_criteria.rationale: Rival A (substrate limitation) is distinguished: relA-knockout would still drop growth while ppGpp stays low, isolating the signaling vs passive mechanism.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: includes the ppGpp-null (relA/spoT knockout) relaxed-phenotype control to prove causality
stringent_criteria.rationale: Includes a relA-knockout design requiring abolished ppGpp surge and blunted growth drop; SpoT not knocked out but relA control is present and canonical.

results.comment: Textbook stringent-response hypothesis, fully operationalized onto in-model observables with quantitative thresholds, time-resolved statistics, a relA-knockout causal control, and both discriminating rivals. Only minor gap: relA/spoT double knockout not tested (Rival B partially relies on SpoT compensation), but the relA control alone satisfies S4. Passes all criteria."}

hypothesis.question: What does a cell do when it suddenly runs out of amino acids?
hypothesis.claim: When intracellular amino acid availability suddenly collapses (mimicked by an abrupt amino-acid withdrawal timeline), E. coli triggers the RelA-dependent stringent response: uncharged tRNA accumulation drives a rapid, large spike in ppGpp, which in turn suppresses ribosome concentration/rRNA synthesis and causes an acute drop in growth rate, all of which is abolished if RelA is knocked out.
hypothesis.h1: Sudden amino-acid withdrawal causes a RelA-dependent surge in ppgpp_conc (>=5-fold over basal within ~10 min) that precedes and causally drives a decline in ribosome_conc and growth_rate; this response requires relA (its knockout abolishes the ppGpp surge and blunts the growth-rate drop).
hypothesis.h0: Amino-acid withdrawal produces no ppGpp surge beyond wildtype/basal noise (fold-change < 2, within replicate CV), and any growth-rate decline is RelA-independent (persists unchanged in a relA knockout).

operational_defs.observable: ppgpp_conc time series after amino-acid withdrawal
operational_defs.measure: peak fold-change in ppgpp_conc relative to pre-shift baseline within first 10-20 min
drop in fraction_trna_charged at shift onset, used to confirm the stimulus was delivered

operational_defs.observable: ribosome_conc and growth_rate time series post-shift
operational_defs.measure: % decline in ribosome_conc and growth_rate relative to pre-shift baseline, measured at 30-60 min post-shift

operational_defs.observable: ppgpp_conc and growth_rate in relA-knockout background under the same shift
operational_defs.measure: comparison of peak ppgpp_conc fold-change and growth_rate decline between relA-knockout and wildtype under identical amino-acid withdrawal

hypothesis.predicted_effect: Within 10-20 min of amino-acid withdrawal, ppgpp_conc rises >=5-fold over basal (target fold-change ~5-10x), followed within 30-60 min by a >=40% drop in ribosome_conc and a >=50% drop in growth_rate relative to pre-shift baseline; in a relA-knockout under the identical shift, the ppgpp_conc fold-change falls to <2x (i.e. reduced by >=60% relative to wildtype's fold-change) and the growth-rate decline is attenuated by >=50% relative to wildtype.

falsifier.decision_rule: Welch t-test on peak ppgpp_conc (0-20 min post-shift window) for amino_acid_shift/basal vs wildtype/basal across seeds; require |t|>=2 AND effect_pct (fold-change) >=5x to support H1. Additionally, compare amino_acid_shift/basal vs gene_knockout(relA)+amino_acid_shift/basal on same channel: require the knockout's fold-change to be <2x and reduced by >=60% relative to wildtype-background shift for RelA-dependence to hold.
falsifier.refuting_result: If amino_acid_shift/basal shows ppgpp_conc fold-change <2x (or |t|<2) relative to wildtype/basal, OR if the relA-knockout background under the same shift shows a ppGpp surge and growth-rate decline statistically indistinguishable from wildtype (|t|<2, fold-change difference <20%), H1 is refuted.

rivals.claim: Rival A: The growth-rate decline is driven primarily by direct substrate limitation (lack of charged tRNA/amino acids for translation elongation) rather than by ppGpp-mediated regulatory shutdown — a purely mechanistic/kinetic effect, not a signaling response.
rivals.distinguishing_result: In the relA-knockout, growth_rate would still drop by a similar magnitude (>=40%) as wildtype even though ppgpp_conc stays low (<2x basal), because the substrate-limitation mechanism does not require ppGpp signaling.

rivals.claim: Rival B: Any observed ppGpp rise is an artifact of general nutrient/energy stress signaling (e.g., via SpoT rather than RelA, responding to carbon/energy status) rather than specifically to uncharged-tRNA sensing by RelA.
rivals.distinguishing_result: A ppgpp_conc surge would still occur in the relA-knockout (comparable fold-change to wildtype, |t|<2 vs wildtype), indicating SpoT or another pathway compensates and the RelA-specific mechanism proposed in H1 is not necessary.

auxiliary_assumptions: The simulator's amino_acid_shift/timeline mechanism for withdrawing amino acids produces an intracellular tRNA-charging deficit analogous to real starvation (i.e., the model computes fraction_trna_charged responsively to media aa removal).

RelA knockout in this model is mechanistically simulated to reduce ppGpp synthesis specifically upon uncharged-tRNA sensing (ribosome-stalling detection), not merely relabeled as inactive without functional consequence.

Growth_rate and ribosome_conc are computed as emergent outputs of the model's regulatory/metabolic network rather than being hard-coded functions of media condition, so a causal chain (tRNA charging -> ppGpp -> ribosome/growth) can in principle be detected.

Seed-to-seed stochastic variation (8 seeds) is sufficient to distinguish signal from intrinsic noise for a Welch t-test at the stated thresholds; if not, additional seeds would be requested downstream.
The withdrawal is applied uniformly to all amino acids simultaneously (not a single-aa limitation), consistent with a 'sudden run out of amino acids' scenario described in the question.

results.question: Which genes can E. coli live without?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names post-KO growth rate/viability vs wild-type as the observable
min_criteria.rationale: Primary observable is steady-state growth_rate (generations 5-20) for each knockout vs wildtype/basal via Welch t-test and effect_pct.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts most single knockouts viable, a minority lethal, vs a baseline
min_criteria.rationale: It predicts two specific non-essential genes viable and one rRNA-operon KO lethal, but explicitly scopes to tested loci and does NOT claim most single KOs viable / a minority lethal genome-wide vs a baseline.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a falsifier (a wrong essential/dispensable call vs literature in the matched medium)
min_criteria.rationale: Falsifier: if acrB/ybeD show >=10% deficit or rRNA KO fails >=40% deficit, the dispensable/essential calls are wrong; matched to basal minimal_GLC medium.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is a single-gene knockout (or screen) across seeds, in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: Designs are single-gene knockouts (acrB, ybeD) plus rRNA operon KO, 8 seeds each, using in-envelope perturbations.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: states an essential-fraction range (~300-620 of ~4300) and a viability/growth-rate cutoff
stringent_criteria.rationale: No essential-fraction range given; explicitly disclaims genome-wide classification. Growth-rate cutoffs (10%/40%) are stated but the fraction is absent.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: specifies a per-gene test across seeds with a defined threshold
stringent_criteria.rationale: Per-gene Welch t-test across 8 seeds with defined thresholds (|t|>=2, |effect_pct|>=10% for non-essential; >=40% for essential).

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the prediction discriminates condition-dependent from context-free essentiality
stringent_criteria.rationale: Includes an acrB/acetate arm to test conditional dispensability vs unconditional, distinguishing condition-dependent from context-free essentiality.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: specifies the medium (rich vs minimal) because essentiality is condition-dependent
stringent_criteria.rationale: Medium specified as basal minimal_GLC (and an acetate condition), with condition-dependence explicitly motivating the acetate arm.

results.comment: Strong operationalization onto growth_rate with per-gene seeded Welch tests, matched medium, and a condition-dependence arm. Falls short of the canonical scope: it deliberately restricts to 3 loci rather than predicting most-viable/minority-lethal genome-wide (M2) and gives no essential-fraction estimate (S1).

hypothesis.question: Which genes can E. coli live without?
hypothesis.claim: Gene dispensability in this simulator is locus-class-dependent and resolvable via steady-state growth_rate: a small panel of genes annotated as non-essential in prior corpus classification (acrB, an efflux pump; and a second non-essential gene, ybeD, a stress-regulon component) can each be deleted individually without impairing steady-state growth_rate, whereas deletion of ribosomal RNA operon capacity (rrna_operon_knockout) collapses growth. This scopes the claim to the tested loci only — it is a demonstration that the simulator CAN discriminate dispensable-vs-essential genes for representative cases, not a general classifier of all E. coli genes.
hypothesis.h1: Deletion of either of two independently annotated non-essential genes (acrB; and a second non-essential gene, ybeD) leaves steady-state growth_rate within noise of wildtype (|effect_pct| < 10%, Welch t < 2, for BOTH genes individually), while deletion of ribosomal RNA operon capacity (rrna_operon_knockout) produces a large, statistically robust growth_rate deficit (>=40% reduction, Welch t>=2), confirming the simulator distinguishes dispensable from essential genetic loci and that the acrB result is not an idiosyncratic single-gene artifact.
hypothesis.h0: Either (a) at least one of the two nominally non-essential single-gene knockouts (acrB or ybeD) produces a growth_rate change >=10% and Welch t>=2 vs wildtype, indicating the 'non-essential' classification is not resolved by growth_rate, OR (b) rrna_operon_knockout fails to produce a >=40% growth_rate deficit vs wildtype, i.e. the simulator does not differentially resolve gene essentiality via growth_rate.

operational_defs.observable: steady-state specific growth_rate time series across generations post-knockout, assessed for TWO independent non-essential genes
operational_defs.measure: mean growth_rate over generations 5-20 (post-transient) for gene_knockout(acrB)/basal and gene_knockout(ybeD)/basal, each compared to wildtype/basal via Welch t-test and effect_pct
mean growth_rate over generations 5-20 for rrna_operon_knockout/basal compared to wildtype/basal

operational_defs.construct: physiological stress/compensation (secondary diagnostic, not primary falsifier)
operational_defs.measure: mean values over generations 5-20 compared across designs; used only as descriptive diagnostic to flag hidden fitness cost or to characterize (not adjudicate) the solver-artifact rival, since ribosome_conc and growth_rate may derive from the same coupled kinetic-FBA computation in this instrument

hypothesis.predicted_effect: acrB knockout: growth_rate effect_pct ~0% (|effect_pct|<10%, Welch t<2 vs wildtype). ybeD knockout: growth_rate effect_pct ~0% (|effect_pct|<10%, Welch t<2 vs wildtype). rrna_operon_knockout: growth_rate effect_pct <= -40% (e.g. from ~1.0/h to <=0.6/h), Welch t>=2 vs wildtype. Requiring concordance across TWO non-essential genes rules out single-gene idiosyncrasy as the explanation for near-zero effect.

falsifier.decision_rule: Welch t-test of mean growth_rate (generations 5-20) for EACH of gene_knockout/basal(acrB) and gene_knockout/basal(ybeD) vs wildtype/basal; reject H1's dispensability claim if EITHER shows |t|>=2 or |effect_pct|>=10%. Separately, Welch t-test for rrna_operon_knockout/basal vs wildtype/basal; reject H1's essentiality claim if |t|<2 or |effect_pct|<40%.
falsifier.refuting_result: Either non-essential-gene knockout shows |effect_pct|>=10% and |t|>=2 (revealing the acrB-neutral result was gene-specific idiosyncrasy rather than a general dispensability signature), OR rrna_operon_knockout fails to show >=40% growth_rate drop (ribosome capacity loss not resolved as essential) — any of these outcomes falsifies H1 as stated.

rivals.claim: acrB's neutrality is a poor/non-representative single-gene example: many nominally non-essential genes cluster near zero effect trivially, so an n=1 test cannot support a general dispensability pattern even if it supports essentiality via rRNA.
rivals.distinguishing_result: The ybeD knockout (independent locus, different functional class than acrB) shows a growth_rate effect_pct >=10% or Welch t>=2, meaning the near-zero acrB result does not generalize and dispensability calls are gene-idiosyncratic rather than reflecting a coherent non-essential locus class.

rivals.claim: acrB knockout is truly neutral at the growth_rate level but imposes a hidden metabolic/stress cost that only manifests under efflux-dependent stress conditions (e.g. acetate media), not basal — growth_rate alone under basal conditions is an incomplete readout of dispensability.
rivals.distinguishing_result: Running acrB knockout under condition=acetate shows a significant growth_rate or ppgpp_conc deviation from wildtype/acetate that was absent in basal, revealing conditional dispensability rather than unconditional dispensability.

rivals.claim: the simulator's essentiality calls for rrna_operon_knockout reflect an FBA solver infeasibility artifact rather than genuine physiological collapse; however, because ribosome_conc/fraction_trna_charged may be computed from the same coupled kinetic-FBA state as growth_rate in this instrument, this rival can only be partially distinguished — treated here as a noted instrument-limit, not a fully resolvable check.
rivals.distinguishing_result: If fba_objective flags infeasibility concurrently with the growth_rate collapse and ribosome_conc/fraction_trna_charged decline proportionally (expected under both genuine-biology and coupled-artifact scenarios), the test cannot cleanly separate solver-artifact from biology; this is recorded as an auxiliary-assumption caveat rather than a decisive falsifier for this rival.

auxiliary_assumptions: The simulator implements a mechanistic (kinetic + FBA-coupled) representation of rRNA operon deletion sufficient to reduce ribosome_conc, not merely a stoichiometric flux-balance shortcut.
acrB and ybeD deletions in the model do not have unmodeled synthetic-lethal interactions absent from the corpus (seed-to-seed variance is treated as intrinsic noise, not a confound).
Growth_rate over generations 5-20 has reached a post-transient steady state comparable across designs (initial generations 0-4 excluded as transient).

8 seeds is sufficient for Welch t-test power to detect a 10% vs 40% effect_pct distinction given expected per-seed growth_rate CV in this simulator (assumed <=5%, per corpus norms) — if seed CV is much larger this may need revisiting downstream.

The claim is explicitly scoped to the tested loci (acrB, ybeD, rRNA operons) and is NOT asserted to generalize to all E. coli genes; broader genome-wide dispensability classification is out of scope for this design.

ribosome_conc and fraction_trna_charged are used only as descriptive/diagnostic channels for the solver-artifact rival, given the acknowledged instrument limitation that they may be derived from the same coupled computation as growth_rate.
